title: Key concepts: development of new trading cities
description: Check your knowledge on trading cities!
topic_path: Key concepts: development of new trading cities

assessment_items.question_content:
The process of a city increasing in population and more people moving into cities is called

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The process of a city increasing in population is not called a “sultanate.”
A strait is not a land bridge that forms over a river.

choices.content: natural land bridge that forms over a river or a canal
body of water that connects China to India
narrow choke point that connects two bigger bodies of water

assessment_items.question_content:
Hangzhou’s location made it a trading city because

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Niger River is located in Africa.

choices.content: it was located along the Niger River
it linked China to international trade routes
it was situated along the Strait of Malacca

assessment_items.question_content:
One reason Muslim scholars settled in Timbuktu was

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Muslim scholars were welcomed in Timbuktu.

choices.content: the Mali government protected them and encouraged them to come to the city
it was one of the only cities where paper was available
it was built in a similar style to the city of Baghdad and they felt at home there

assessment_items.question_content:
According to the article, which trading city prospered partly because of their control over the body of water nearest them?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Hangzhou is near the East China Sea, but the Song Dynasty didn’t have total control over that huge body of water.

assessment_items.question_content:
According to the article, one way that empires used their trading cities to their advantage was to

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Although some rulers may have installed spy networks, the article doesn’t discuss this as a main way empires used trading cities to their advantage.

choices.content: require the inhabitants of the cities to follow the same religion, which helped ensure peace
install a network of spies in the sections of the cities where merchants lived
tax the goods that flowed in and out of those cities
